title: Operator MFA and session integrity monitoring

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for 6.4.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SWIFT CSP 6.4 (Logging and monitoring) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.34.4: Operator MFA and session integrity monitoring.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.34.4' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: SWIFT Alliance/Lite2 transaction logs, operator session audit trails, network infrastructure logs, endpoint security logs, privileged access management, change management records, vulnerability scan outputs, multi-factor authentication logs
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=cyberark sourcetype="cyberark:pta" OR index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" earliest=-7d
| stats count by user, action, src
| search user="swift*" (action="Logon" OR Operation="UserLoggedIn")
| eval mfa=if(match(_raw,"MFA"),1,0)
| where mfa=0
description: Produces SWIFT Customer Security Programme evidence for operator mfa and session integrity monitoring ahead of independent assessment.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SWIFT CSP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes cyberark, o365 and sourcetypes cyberark:pta, ms:o365:management. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Segment SWIFT CSP evidence into dedicated indexes with strict role-based access. (2) Join operators to HR screening and vendor risk lookups refreshed weekly. (3) Validate MFA and PAM sourcetypes are CIM-mapped for Authentication models. (4) Retain evidence for at least 13 months aligned to attestation cycles. Indexes required: `index=cyberark`, `index=o365`.
visualization: Table (operators), Single value (sessions), Bar chart (CSCF controls). Key fields: `action`, `mfa`, `src`, `user`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Operator sessions from authorized payment operations, scheduled message corrections, or vendor-supervised maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: SWIFT Alliance/Lite2 transaction logs, operator session audit trails, network infrastructure logs, endpoint security logs, privileged access management, change management records, vulnerability scan outputs, multi-factor authentication logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Segment SWIFT CSP evidence into dedicated indexes with strict role-based access. (2) Join operators to HR screening and vendor risk lookups refreshed weekly. (3) Validate MFA and PAM sourcetypes are CIM-mapped for Authentication models. (4) Retain evidence for at least 13 months aligned to attestation cycles.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cyberark sourcetype="cyberark:pta" OR index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" earliest=-7d
| stats count by user, action, src
| search user="swift*" (action="Logon" OR Operation="UserLoggedIn")
| eval mfa=if(match(_raw,"MFA"),1,0)
| where mfa=0
```

Understanding this SPL

**Operator MFA and session integrity monitoring (SWIFT CSCF mandatory)** — Produces SWIFT Customer Security Programme evidence for operator mfa and session integrity monitoring ahead of independent assessment.

Documented **Data sources**: SWIFT Alliance/Lite2 transaction logs, operator session audit trails, network infrastructure logs, endpoint security logs, privileged access management, change management records, vulnerability scan outputs, multi-factor authentication logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cyberark, o365; **sourcetype**: cyberark:pta, ms:o365:management. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cyberark, index=o365, sourcetype="cyberark:pta", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, action, src** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **mfa** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where mfa=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Operator MFA and session integrity monitoring (SWIFT CSCF mandatory)** — Produces SWIFT Customer Security Programme evidence for operator mfa and session integrity monitoring ahead of independent assessment.

Documented **Data sources**: SWIFT Alliance/Lite2 transaction logs, operator session audit trails, network infrastructure logs, endpoint security logs, privileged access management, change management records, vulnerability scan outputs, multi-factor authentication logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register; for access or operator activity, also compare with the source-of-truth for SWIFT application sessions in your environment.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (operators), Single value (sessions), Bar chart (CSCF controls).
grandmaExplanation: We help you show SWIFT security programme expectations are met for your payment zone, operators, and controls before the independent check.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Authentication (CIM data model).
